mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly growth in real GDP and rising retail sales indicate synchronized demand expansion that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative forward returns in >50% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side contractions that force firms to cut capital expenditures, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate erodes real yields and prompts market pricing of imminent monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged
failure_condition: Equity markets generate positive returns in >65% of months where inflation rises without corresponding rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand aggregate household income and corporate revenue baselines, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure falling below 850,000 units signals a collapse in residential investment demand that drags down construction sector earnings and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in >70% of months where housing starts fall below the 850,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of high capacity utilization signals impending supply bottlenecks that compress profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields positive equity returns in >60% of high-capacity regimes over a 4-year backtest.
